game_info.title: Untitled Game
game_info.concept:
Mini Motorways is a strategy simulation game about designing the road map for a growing city. Build a traffic network, one road at a time, to create a bustling metropolis. Redesign your roads and place your motorways to get everyone where they need to go. How long can you keep the city moving?

Have you ever been stuck in traffic and wished you could do something to fix it?

In Mini Motorways, the city’s traffic problems are in your hands.

From the makers of Mini Metro, Mini Motorways is a game about drawing the roads that drive a growing city. Build a road network, one road at a time, to create a bustling metropolis. Redesign your city to keep the traffic flowing, and carefully manage upgrades to meet the changing demands. How long can you keep the cities of the world moving?

Key Features

  • Draw roads in dynamic cities that grow and change—every game is unique!

  • Explore stunning maps inspired by cities all around the world.

  • Choose from a variety of upgrades to meet the demands of your ever-expanding road network: highways, roundabouts, and more!

  • Build without limits in Creative Mode—design your dream city exactly how you want it with unlimited upgrades and editing tools.

  • Stress-free Endless Mode offers you a relaxing gameplay experience, plus you can continue building your city after Game Over.

  • Tackle our Expert Mode, which features twists such as permanent roads and limited upgrades for the ultimate challenge.

  • Select different colour palettes on every map, including Colourblind and Night Modes.

  • Relax to a responsive soundtrack from Disasterpeace that grows along with your city.

  • Unlock new maps and achievements as you achieve mastery.

  • Export GIFs of your city layouts to share with your friends.

  • Compete against other players in Daily and Weekly Challenges.
game_info.description: Build a thriving city by connecting colorful houses to their matching destination buildings. Draw roads to create efficient routes, but watch out—traffic builds up quickly! Manage your limited road tiles wisely and use special upgrades like highways to keep cars moving. The city grows over time with new houses and destinations appearing. Keep traffic flowing and prevent any building from getting too backed up, or it's game over!
game_info.controls:
Arrow Keys: Move cursor to select road placement positions
Space: Place/remove road tile at cursor position
Shift: Activate upgrade mode to place highway tiles (when available)
Z: Toggle between road placement and building connection view
Enter: Start game from start screen
Escape: Pause/unpause game
R: Restart and return to start screen

TEST_1.test_description: Basic movement and road placement testing. Validates that the cursor can move around the grid and that roads can be placed and removed correctly. Tests the fundamental mechanics of the game.
TEST_1.strategy_description: Move cursor in all four directions systematically. Place roads in different patterns and configurations. Test road removal by placing and then removing roads. Verify grid boundaries are respected.
TEST_1.expected_outcome: Cursor moves correctly within grid bounds. Roads are placed at correct positions and can be removed. No errors or crashes occur during basic interactions. Grid state updates properly.

TEST_1.expected: Cursor moves correctly within grid bounds. Roads are placed at correct positions and can be removed. No errors or crashes occur during basic interactions. Grid state updates properly.

TEST_2.test_description: Optimal strategy to win the game by efficiently connecting houses to destinations. Tests pathfinding, traffic management, and strategic road placement to keep the city running as long as possible.
TEST_2.strategy_description: Prioritize connecting houses with highest demand first. Create direct routes between matching houses and destinations. Use highways strategically on high-traffic routes. Monitor building queues and adapt road network. Expand connections as new buildings appear.
TEST_2.expected_outcome: Successfully maintains traffic flow for extended period. Reaches high score by preventing building overload. Demonstrates effective road network design. Eventually achieves GAME_OVER when city becomes unmanageable, validating difficulty curve.

TEST_3.test_description: Tests highway upgrade system and validates that special tiles work correctly. Ensures upgrades are earned properly and can be placed strategically to improve traffic flow.
TEST_3.strategy_description: Play until highway upgrades are unlocked. Place highways on existing busy roads. Verify cars move faster on highway tiles. Test that highways connect properly with regular roads.
TEST_3.expected_outcome: Highway upgrades unlock at appropriate times. Highway tiles function correctly with faster car movement. Upgrade counter decrements properly. Traffic flow improves on upgraded routes.
TEST_3.description: Tests highway upgrade system and validates that special tiles work correctly. Ensures upgrades are earned properly and can be placed strategically to improve traffic flow.

TEST_3.expected: Highway upgrades unlock at appropriate times. Highway tiles function correctly with faster car movement. Upgrade counter decrements properly. Traffic flow improves on upgraded routes.

TEST_4.test_description: Stress test with rapid building spawning and multiple simultaneous connections needed. Tests game stability under pressure and validates queue management system.
TEST_4.strategy_description: Allow game to progress until multiple houses and destinations exist. Create suboptimal routes to generate traffic congestion. Monitor building queues growing. Test game's ability to handle multiple cars simultaneously.
TEST_4.expected_outcome: Game remains stable with many entities. Queue system accurately tracks waiting cars. Game over triggers when a building's queue exceeds limit. No performance issues or visual glitches occur.
TEST_4.description: Stress test with rapid building spawning and multiple simultaneous connections needed. Tests game stability under pressure and validates queue management system.
TEST_4.strategy: Allow game to progress until multiple houses and destinations exist. Create suboptimal routes to generate traffic congestion. Monitor building queues growing. Test game's ability to handle multiple cars simultaneously.
TEST_4.expected: Game remains stable with many entities. Queue system accurately tracks waiting cars. Game over triggers when a building's queue exceeds limit. No performance issues or visual glitches occur.

TEST_5.test_description: Tests edge cases like attempting to place roads on occupied tiles, removing critical roads, and handling disconnected networks. Validates error handling and game logic robustness.
TEST_5.strategy_description: Attempt to place roads on buildings and existing roads. Remove roads that cars are currently using. Create isolated road segments. Test placing roads with insufficient tiles remaining.
TEST_5.expected_outcome: Game prevents invalid placements gracefully. Cars handle road removal by stopping or rerouting. UI provides clear feedback for invalid actions. Resource management works correctly.
TEST_5.description: Tests edge cases like attempting to place roads on occupied tiles, removing critical roads, and handling disconnected networks. Validates error handling and game logic robustness.

TEST_5.expected: Game prevents invalid placements gracefully. Cars handle road removal by stopping or rerouting. UI provides clear feedback for invalid actions. Resource management works correctly.
